verdicts.reasoning: Both dates are defensible but refer to different events. The pipeline's 2013-10-14 is the date the merger agreement was executed ('on October 14, 2013, the merger agreement was executed'). Alex's 2013-09-21 is the date CSC/Pamplona confirmed they would increase their price to $21.25 ('On September 21, 2013, the members of the Transaction Committee called the representative of CSC/Pamplona... Later that same day, the representative of CSC/Pamplona called the members of the Transaction Committee to confirm that CSC/Pamplona were willing to increase their all-cash purchase price to $21.25 per share'). These are different valid milestone dates for the winning bid. The pipeline treats the formal execution as the 'executed' event; Alex treats the oral price confirmation as the definitive bid event. Both are textually supported.

The filing states Party A offered '$17.00 to $19.00 per share.' The pipeline correctly records this as a range (range_low=17.00, range_high=19.00) with no single per_share value, so value is null. Alex records 17.0, using the low end of the range as the representative value. Both are defensible conventions for encoding a range bid. The pipeline is more precise; Alex follows a common analyst convention of using the range floor.

The filing describes this as an 'unsolicited proposal' submitted before any formal process was initiated. At this stage no management presentation, data room access, or formal bid instructions had been issued. The filing later describes the Special Committee formalizing its review process and inviting indications of interest, making this initial approach clearly informal/unsolicited. 'Informal' is the correct classification. The pipeline's 'Uncertain' is overly conservative.

The filing states Party A submitted a revised indication of interest with '$18.00 to $19.00 per share.' The pipeline correctly records this as a range (range_low=18.00, range_high=19.00) with no single value. Alex records 18.0 (the range floor). Same convention difference as the June 21 proposal -- both are defensible.

The filing states Party A 'reiterated in a telephone call... that its previous indication of interest with an all-cash purchase price of $18.00 to $19.00 per share was its best and final offer.' The pipeline records the range (18.00-19.00) with null single value. Alex records 18.0 as the range floor. Same convention difference.

The filing describes this September 18 event as Party A 'reiterated in a telephone call to a representative of BofA Merrill Lynch that its previous indication of interest... was its best and final offer.' This was an oral reiteration via telephone, not a formal written submission. Additionally, unlike CSC/Pamplona which submitted a formal written proposal with commitment letters on this date, Party A merely restated its prior range by phone. 'Informal' is the correct classification for an oral telephone reiteration.

Both dates are textually supported but represent different interpretations of the 'drop' event. The pipeline uses 2013-09-19, the date of the Special Committee meeting where the committee concluded CSC/Pamplona had the best proposal and effectively decided to proceed with CSC/Pamplona exclusively, implicitly rejecting Party A. Alex uses 2013-09-24, the date the exclusivity agreement with CSC/Pamplona was formally executed, which formally excluded all other bidders. The pipeline's date captures the decision point; Alex's date captures the formal execution of exclusivity. Both are valid interpretations of when Party A was effectively dropped.

The filing states Party B submitted a preliminary indication of interest at '$17.00 to $18.00 per share.' The pipeline correctly records this as a range (range_low=17.00, range_high=18.00) with null single value. Alex records 17.0 as the range floor. Same convention difference as other range bids.

Same reasoning as Party A drop date. Pipeline uses 2013-09-19 (Special Committee decision meeting where CSC/Pamplona was selected as preferred bidder). Alex uses 2013-09-24 (formal execution of exclusivity agreement with CSC/Pamplona). Both are valid interpretations of when Party B was effectively dropped from the process.

The filing states Party C presented 'an oral preliminary indication of interest at an all-cash purchase price of $15.00 to $17.00 per share.' The pipeline correctly records this as a range (range_low=15.00, range_high=17.00) with null single value. Alex records 15.0 as the range floor. Same convention difference.

The filing states Party C submitted 'a written indication of interest at an all-cash purchase price of $16.00 to $16.50 per share.' The pipeline correctly records this as a range (range_low=16.00, range_high=16.50) with null single value. Alex records 16.0 as the range floor. Same convention difference.

The filing states Party C submitted 'a revised oral indication of interest with an all-cash purchase price of $16.00 to $17.00 per share.' The pipeline correctly records this as a range (range_low=16.00, range_high=17.00) with null single value. Alex records 16.0 as the range floor. Same convention difference.

The filing explicitly states: 'June 30, 2013, Party C entered into a confidentiality and standstill agreement with Mac-Gray and was provided with an informational package.' The pipeline's date of 2013-06-30 matches the filing text exactly. Alex's date of 2013-06-20 has no support in the filing text for a Party C NDA event. This appears to be a data entry error in Alex's benchmark.
